tutorials.title: Section 1: Start Here
tutorials.description: Use this opening section to understand the guide structure, the core author context, and when to use each later section.

tutorials.title: Lab 1: WMS lifecycle, QA, and publishing flow
tutorials.description: Explains workshop statuses, approval handoffs, stakeholder QA, and publishing flow before authors begin the hands-on workflow.

tutorials.title: Lab 2: GitHub foundations
tutorials.description: Explains the Oracle LiveLabs GitHub model, including repositories, forks, clones, and the safe authoring flow before authors start execution labs.

tutorials.title: Section 2: Core Workflow
tutorials.description: Use this section for the hands-on author path from workshop request through GitHub setup, repository sync, and Markdown development.

tutorials.title: Lab 3: Submit new workshop in WMS
tutorials.description: Walks authors through the workshop request, required metadata, ownership fields, and WMS workflow setup.

tutorials.title: Lab 4: Set up GitHub and install tools
tutorials.description: Covers GitHub account setup, GitHub Desktop, Visual Studio Code, and the core extensions most authors need.

tutorials.title: Lab 5: Stay in sync with GitHub
tutorials.description: Shows authors how to fork, clone, sync, structure their repository, and preview workshop content safely.

tutorials.title: Lab 6: Develop Markdown and content
tutorials.description: Covers workshop structure, manifests, shared content reuse, Markdown rules, images, and validation-oriented authoring practices.

tutorials.title: Section 3: Validation and Publish
tutorials.description: Use this section for review readiness, pull request checks, self-QA, publishing, and timeline expectations.

tutorials.title: Lab 7: QA checks and steps
tutorials.description: Explains how to share previews, complete self-QA, handle quarterly QA, and prepare the workshop for stakeholder review.

tutorials.title: Lab 8: Review, QA, and publishing timelines
tutorials.description: Summarizes expected turnaround times for review, stakeholder QA, publishing, and support follow-up.

tutorials.title: Lab 9: Pull Request automated checks
tutorials.description: Explains the automated checks that can block a pull request and how to interpret their output.

tutorials.title: Lab 10: Publish your workshop
tutorials.description: Walks authors through the final pull request and WMS publishing steps that move content into production.

tutorials.title: Section 4: Reuse and Enhancements
tutorials.description: Optional components and reusable patterns that extend a workshop after the core flow is in place.

tutorials.title: Lab 11: Embed SQL Developer with FreeSQL
tutorials.description: Shows how to embed a FreeSQL editor directly into a workshop task when that interaction adds value.

tutorials.title: Lab 12: Add LiveLabs quizzes
tutorials.description: Shows how to add quizzes and knowledge checks to a LiveLabs workshop.

tutorials.title: Section 5: Tools and Productivity
tutorials.description: Use these labs when you need better screenshots, lighter images, or content-cleanup help while authoring.

tutorials.title: Lab 13: Capture effective screens
tutorials.description: Covers screenshot best practices so workshop steps are visually clear and reviewable.

tutorials.title: Lab 14: Optimize screenshots with OptiShot
tutorials.description: Shows authors how to reduce image size and keep screenshots clean without losing readability.

tutorials.title: Lab 15: Clean up content with Fixomat
tutorials.description: Explains how to use Fixomat for Markdown cleanup and asset optimization before review or QA.

tutorials.title: Section 6: Specialized Workflows
tutorials.description: Advanced or niche workflows that only apply when a workshop needs sprint, remote desktop, or Marketplace-specific steps.

tutorials.title: Lab 16: Develop LiveLabs Sprints
tutorials.description: Shows how to structure, author, preview, and publish LiveLabs Sprint content.

tutorials.title: Lab 17: Set up graphical remote desktop
tutorials.description: Explains how to configure and use a graphical remote desktop for a workshop environment.

tutorials.title: Lab 18: Create custom image for Marketplace
tutorials.description: Explains how to create and prepare a Marketplace image for workshop use.

tutorials.title: Lab 19: Publish your image to Oracle Marketplace
tutorials.description: Shows how to publish a prepared image to Oracle Marketplace.

tutorials.title: Lab 20: Update the image on your sandbox environment
tutorials.description: Shows how to register a published Marketplace listing and update the image used by a LiveLabs sandbox environment.

tutorials.title: Lab 21: Secure Desktop environments in LiveLabs
tutorials.description: Explains when OCI Secure Desktops make sense for LiveLabs workshops and what they change in delivery.

tutorials.title: Lab 22: Request and access Secure Desktop environments
tutorials.description: Explains how to request and access OCI Secure Desktop environments for a LiveLabs workshop.

tutorials.title: Section 7: Help and FAQ
tutorials.description: Use this section for support paths and common authoring questions when you are blocked or need a quick answer.

tutorials.title: Need Help?
tutorials.description: Explains where workshop authors should go for support, office hours, email help, and Slack assistance.

tutorials.title: LiveLabs FAQ
tutorials.description: Collects common questions about LiveLabs production, development, workshop structure, and event workflows.
